author: Tomorrow tomorrow2
title: Flexible and functional playback app!
content: This is an app worth paying for - I use it every day. It’s seen continued development and works really well. If there were two features I’d love to see, it would be the option to drill down and play just a specific disk of an album, and some form of smart playlists. I use Evermusic for audio based education rather than music, so handling various forms of metadata has been one of its real strengths.

author: thedreamingsentinel
title: Such a buggy app
content: Won’t stop shuffling songs, even playlists. When playing music in a queue it won’t stop shuffling after the first 2. It’s also grabbing (or A.I generating?) random weird art for all my songs. I thought this app was nice because I don’t need to sync my phone to my computer to add .mp3 files and edit details. But I have come to hate it so much.

author: All apps are a ripoff
title: Not simple
content:
I’ve been using iPhones since iPhone 4. This app is almost impossible to navigate. There was a bold statement that this app could play files directly from an iXpand Sandisk drive. OK…. How? Nothing I have looked at really helps. Possibly a waste of $22.00 I will update if something clicks, but don’t hold your breath. Edit: The page that describes how to utilise a Sandisk iPhone drive isn’t helpful, either is support for this app, who never replied to my query. Thanks for the help <= Sarcasm. EDIT: I have requested a refund from Apple.

Edit: I have been refunded. Thank you Apple.

Very disappointing, especially after paying $14.99 with the hope I’d be receiving a better, more premium experience.

author: Cammac1297
title: Playlist Issues
content:
Whenever I add music to my playlist it adds it on the bottom of all the files and not the top! I have to go to sort and press (by modification date) 2x and then it will come up on top of all the files.

Also recently added files get randomly removed from my playlist after a day or 2 and i have to add them back to the playlist again! Please fix.

author: Nosarh
title: For Reorganise my music
content: The best app I found to play the music I want from the files of my iphone. Great audio player with many features. I really need to loop audios usually and this is the app to use to share memos and repeat them in loop. As an artist I think is great.

author: Allan@JP
title: Best offline player I’ve used
content:
Evermusic has proved to be the best offline music player yet for me. It allows imports from Apple Music, iTunes as well as computers or online streams. After years of putting up with Apple Music dropping out and variable quality of playback I’m impressed to find an app that streams well to my hearing aids without the previous fluctuations of sound levels and quality. An added feature is the ability to sort alphabetically or by artists etc and a high quality search engine.

After seeing the options available I was happy to purchase the premium app after only using the free app for a couple of hours.

All I can say is keep up the great work.

author: 3.5 star

content:
It is great for iphone as it can run in background means screen black wont stop playing.
Next would hope can play video so that I can see the lyrics

author: yulumie
title: All good
content: Still going good and not once let me down yet since early this year

author: Jack the iPhone expert
title: Does not store the last file position
content: I mainly use Evermusic to listen to audio books. However it does not store the last file and position. Need to search each time.

author: Roly D59
title: The Best
content: A flawless App integrates old schooled ripped and contemporary streaming 6 stars

author: Jayden-Jones
title: Hmmmm
content: So I’m giving this app a go but I can’t understand for the life of my why Dropbox sync is SLLLOOOWWW!!?? Why? Been an hour and not one song has showed up in the list so far.

author: Xivyangel
title: Does not Sync playlist across all devices
content: This App could have been good if only the playlist you’ve created synchronizes to all your devices. I emailed the customer service but no one get back to me with an answer about the synchronisation. So, also a negative rating for customer service.

author: Troz 166
title: Works OK, lacks a couple of things
content:
I intend to use this app just to play music in offline mode, ie files stored on the phone. 

It took me a while to get it working - the “Wi-Fi drive” feature did not work for me. However it found the NAS on my home network and I was able to download files from there. 

What I would like to do is use it like I play music on my computer. My music is organised in folders grouped by style, era and so forth. So I would like to be able to navigate quickly through the folders hierarchy and play everything in a folder. But if you do that in this app it plays the tracks in alphabetical order, not sorted by the track number tag. 

You can play tracks in the right order if you play albums from the “Album” tab, like on an old fashioned iPod, so that is acceptable. 

I queried this through the support form in the app and an answer came back in a few hours, so good developer support.

author: moni🧚
title: Best
content: Thank you 🤍

author: spidy3210
title: Good but ads are there
content: It’s ok that at least I can play songs from a music player with free version

author: @nomimous
title: Good App by Good People
content: Good App by Good People is what you’d say when you use this app. Clearly the team behind this team is headed down the right path. Ad are minimal but effective and not annoying, but please don't increase it. I wish the team many success for their efforts💪🏼

author: ЯΣƧidΣПƬ
title: Best Free Music Player
content: Been searching for a free music player as replacement for Apple Music, since they forced users to subscribe to play content. After test driving a dozen different apps over the past few months, I finally found this one. Initially a little confused with set up, but after uploading my iTunes collection to free cloud service Box using my iMac, I had access to my complete collection on my iPhone in a just a few minutes. So impressed they included so many features for free, including ability to set up customised playlists, change user interface, compatible with CarPlay to use in my car, as well as access to other cloud accounts when I finally eventually run out of 10Gb free space on Box. Considering what you get for free, this is quite simply the best option for someone like me who just wants to enjoy the music I have without buying into an expensive subscription services that rarely give users value for money.

author: tiktokisnotthatgoodbutok
title: Very good app
content: Although sometimes glitchy and thus crashing, I don't mind very much! I find that this app allows me to not only to download songs, but as well as to change the cover's images to my liking, make playlists, download music from my apple music and best of all, when I cannot find the specific artist who sang this song, the app does it for me! And it does it correctly too! This is a very good app!

author: back1260
title: We need a widget asap
content: Otherwise cool app since we can download music and listen to it without the need to put it in itunes.

content: Reasonable app but let down by not supporting Apple family subscription sharing

author: bejeldc
content: this app is trash and i don’t got the patience for it 🤦‍♀️🤦‍♀️

author: LaMelo#1
title: Amazing
content: I was looking for an app to put my downloaded music in, and I found this app, worked in seconds, was amazing, one of the best and most convenient music apps ever.

author: Wildemang
title: Seamless playback of flac files
content: If, like me, you were searching for a player that didn’t have that annoying gap between what should have been a seamless playback of a downloaded flac album this is the one you need. I have a few Wild Ambience nature albums that immerse me in forest sounds and the gap between songs was jarring, now no longer with Evermusic. The Pro version at only $7.99 is well worth it for offline listening as well. A great player for iPads.

author: Emily no2
title: Great
content: Good job with the pricing! The only really amazing music playing app I’ve found thus far. Few premium features, but with a once off fee thought I could do it. Well done Evermusic

content: Good overall player. A bit buggy. Often stops playing when switching apps. Need to close and restart it. Fix this, and add Siri support and ability to share playlists with another device, and I would rate it 5 stars.

title: Nice app
content: It's s a complete package.

author: Xz547
title: Fix this problem
content: I can not download music from my icloud is it because of update? Please fix this I can’t download the music now...

author: unfriendlyfriend
title: Missing 2 stars
content:
Tried different types of offline music players and Evermusic turned out to be flawless but was lagged in two main points

1) where is airplay???. The app does have share music but that doesn’t work with airplay devices like home pod . Please add airplay option which is very very useful for most of the people who are in Apple ecosystem.

2) backgrounds : the ambience of the player would be much beautiful if we could have the option of choosing our own photo backgrounds like other apps say Melodista. I am not comparing but just letting the team know these two points makes the app complete .

I am one step away to become a subscription member . Those above two points

author: L3V1 D1R33N
title: DOWNLOAD NOW!!!
content: It’s the title

author: R0b Riff
title: Brilliant!
content: Finally a music app that actually works! ...and across all platforms, fast as well, love it!

author: Kgr44
title: Best cloud player but sync is slow
content: This is the best cloud player I have found with heaps of great features, but since the last release of the app it seems to take an extra long time to synchronise the cloud library. I have been trying to sync my library for the last three weeks and there are still songs without album covers and songs that have not been populated into the albums, artists, or genres? Is there an update planned to address this?

author: David JH
title: Suite of selectively disabled apps suite of apps that are so very unstable
content:
Crashes constantly trying to sync with a not very large dlna library of around 3000 tracks.

Graphic equaliser UI too small to adjust with any meaningful accuracy 

Everappz producrs a very confusing suite of apps that all seem close to completely identical , but the differences are sooo subtle and just silly. 

To get the full possibility of features you need to actually buy a few different app versions from same developer..eg cross fade is disabled not in Flacbox, but available in Evermusic and evermusic pro. 24bit audio support is available in flacbox but disabled  evermusic or ever music pro., but, Flacbox can’t see locally stored podcasts but the other two evermusic apps can. ??? This incredibly subtle product differentiation seems designed not to produce the best and most comprehensive app, but to force you to buy multiple versions just to have the features you’d need.
